definition: To perform a role or behavior
sentences: The students acted out a scene from the play.

definition: To behave inappropriately or malfunction
sentences: The computer started to act up and froze.

definition: To request or invite someone to do something
sentences: I'm going to ask for help with this difficult task.

definition: To support or assist someone or something
sentences: Can you back up your claims with evidence?

definition: To reverse or move backward
sentences: Could you back up the car so I can park?

definition: To be suited for a particular role or occupation
sentences: She is definitely cut out for a career in medicine.

definition: To explode or burst into pieces
sentences: The fireworks will blow up in the sky tonight.

definition: To lose one's temper or become angry
sentences: He blew up at his colleague during the meeting.

definition: To flee or escape from someone or something physically
sentences: The puppy slipped its leash and tried to break away into the woods.

definition: To separate from a larger group or organization
sentences: The cyclist decided to break away from the main pack and pedal ahead.

definition: To stop working due to a mechanical or technical failure
sentences: My ten-year-old car finally broke down on the highway this morning.

definition: To lose emotional control and start crying
sentences: When she heard the devastating news, she simply broke down and wept.

definition: To divide complex information into smaller, simpler parts
sentences: Can you break down the quarterly expenses so we can easily see where our money went?

definition: To enter a building or place forcibly or illegally
sentences: The thief attempted to break in to the house.

definition: To suddenly stop speaking in the middle of a sentence
sentences: She broke off mid-sentence when the loud thunder rattled the windows.

definition: To end a relationship or conversation abruptly
sentences: After realizing they were fundamentally incompatible, they decided to break off their engagement.

definition: To detach or separate a piece from a larger object
sentences: He broke off a small piece of the chocolate bar and handed it to me.

definition: To escape from confinement or captivity
sentences: The notorious thief managed to break out of the high-security prison last night.

definition: To start suddenly and spread rapidly

definition: To develop a skin rash, acne, or other skin irritation
sentences: She tends to break out in hives whenever she eats strawberries.

definition: To overcome an obstacle or make a significant achievement
sentences: They managed to break through the language barrier.

definition: To bring someone or something with you

definition: To introduce or earn a certain amount of money

definition: To move something from outside into an indoor space

definition: To carry something from inside to outside

definition: To release or publish something to the public

definition: To make a quality or feature more noticeable or vivid

definition: To dismiss or ignore someone or something casually

definition: To develop or expand on something already existing

definition: To require or demand something

definition: To make a telephone call

definition: To become overly excited or emotional in a situation

definition: To complete or perform a task or action

definition: To understand or grasp something, often after a delay

definition: To become widely popular or accepted by many people

definition: To spend time with someone to hear about what is new in their life

definition: To complete tasks or work that you have fallen behind on

definition: To become deeply absorbed or involved in a situation (usually caught up in)

definition: To mark or indicate that something has been completed or verified
sentences: I'll check off the items on the list as we go.

definition: To review or examine something carefully

definition: To happen or occur

definition: To make progress or improve

definition: To become ill with a particular illness or condition

definition: To visit someone's place

I need to hand in my resignation letter.
